description: My primary coding environment — I've customized VS Code with extensions, snippets, and keybindings that make every coding session fast and focused.
Git is the backbone of everything I build — branching strategies, rebasing, conflict resolution, and clean commit history are all part of my daily discipline.
GitHub is my second home — I manage open source projects, review PRs, track issues, and ship code through Actions pipelines every single day.
I use GitLab for self-hosted repositories and enterprise CI/CD workflows — its all-in-one DevOps platform keeps teams aligned from code to deployment.
Containers are central to how I think about software delivery — Docker lets me package apps with confidence that they'll run identically everywhere.
I orchestrate multi-container local environments with Docker Compose — spinning up databases, caches, and APIs together with a single command.
I manage containerized workloads at scale with Kubernetes — handling deployments, rolling updates, autoscaling, and service discovery in production clusters.
Helm charts are my preferred way to package and deploy Kubernetes applications — templated, versioned, and repeatable across every environment.
AWS is my cloud of choice — I architect resilient systems using EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS, and IAM with a strong focus on cost efficiency and security.
I leverage GCP for data-heavy workloads and AI integrations — BigQuery, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI are my most-used services on the platform.
I use Azure for enterprise client deployments — App Services, Azure DevOps, and Active Directory integrations are core to my workflow there.
Cloudflare sits in front of nearly every production app I deploy — handling DNS, SSL, DDoS protection, and edge caching with zero compromise on speed.
I configure Nginx as a battle-hardened reverse proxy and load balancer — fine-tuning rate limiting, SSL termination, and upstream routing for production traffic.
Postman is my API testing command center — I build collections, write test scripts, and set up automated monitors to validate every endpoint I ship.
I document every API I build with Swagger — interactive docs that let teams explore and test endpoints without ever opening a terminal.
I write OpenAPI specs as a contract-first approach to API design — keeping frontend and backend teams in sync before a single line of code is written.
My CI/CD automation engine of choice — I design workflows for testing, building Docker images, deploying to cloud, and releasing packages without human intervention.
I build multi-stage GitLab pipelines with environment-specific variables, Docker-in-Docker builds, and rollback strategies baked right in.
I set up Jenkins pipelines for legacy enterprise systems — plugin management, shared libraries, and Groovy-based Jenkinsfiles are all in my toolkit.
Atlas is my managed MongoDB of choice — I use it for cloud-native deployments with built-in scaling, backups, and Atlas Search for full-text capabilities.
PostgreSQL is my relational database of choice — I design normalized schemas, write complex joins, and tune query performance for data-intensive applications.
Redis is my performance multiplier — I use it for session caching, rate limiting, pub/sub messaging, and slashing database load on high-traffic endpoints.
S3 is my default object storage layer — I use it for file uploads, static asset hosting, backups, and pre-signed URL workflows across dozens of projects.
I craft Grafana dashboards that turn raw metrics into clear operational stories — from CPU spikes to API latency percentiles, nothing stays hidden.
I instrument apps with Prometheus metrics and configure alerting rules that fire before users ever notice something is wrong.
Sentry is my safety net — I integrate it across every project to capture exceptions, trace performance bottlenecks, and resolve issues before they escalate.
I use the ELK Stack to centralize logs from distributed services — Logstash parses, Elasticsearch indexes, and Kibana surfaces the insights I need instantly.
I monitor every production endpoint with UptimeRobot — instant alerts via Slack and email mean downtime gets addressed in minutes, not hours.
I implement GA4 across web apps to track user flows, measure conversions, and give product teams the behavioral data they need to make smart decisions.
I write k6 load test scripts to stress-test APIs under realistic traffic — identifying breaking points and bottlenecks before they hit production.
Security is not an afterthought — I use OWASP ZAP to run automated vulnerability scans and pen tests on every API before it goes anywhere near production.
I use HashiCorp Vault to manage secrets, API keys, and certificates centrally — no more hardcoded credentials scattered across environment files.
I store and rotate sensitive credentials securely using AWS Secrets Manager — integrated directly into Lambda functions and ECS tasks with IAM-controlled access.
Slack is the communication layer of every team I've worked with — I also build Slack integrations and bots that pipe deployment alerts and monitoring events straight into channels.
I live in Jira across sprints — managing backlogs, writing detailed tickets, tracking velocity, and running retrospectives that actually improve how teams work.
I use Zoho for client-facing project management and CRM workflows — its integrated suite keeps communication, tasks, and documents neatly in one place.
From Teams calls to SharePoint docs and Excel-powered reporting, I navigate the full Microsoft 365 ecosystem comfortably in enterprise environments.
Google Workspace powers my daily productivity — Docs, Sheets, Drive, and Meet are tools I use to collaborate, document decisions, and keep projects moving.
I write and maintain technical documentation in Confluence — architecture decisions, runbooks, and onboarding guides that teams actually read and reference.
Notion is my personal knowledge base and project hub — I use it for roadmaps, meeting notes, content planning, and building internal wikis that scale with teams.
I configure PagerDuty escalation policies and on-call schedules so critical incidents reach the right engineer fast — minimizing MTTR across production systems.
Opsgenie keeps my incident response sharp — I set up alert routing, deduplication rules, and team rotations that cut alert noise without missing real issues.
I bridge design and development using Figma — inspecting specs, collaborating on prototypes, and translating pixel-perfect designs into clean, responsive code.
